core.models.baseline_measurement module

BASELINE MEASUREMENT – Layer 2: Idle Reference

This class represents system idle power measurements. Stored separately from raw measurements, never applied directly.

Author: Deepak Panigrahy

class core.models.baseline_measurement.BaselineMeasurement(baseline_id: str, timestamp: float, power_watts: ~typing.Dict[str, float], duration_seconds: float, sample_count: int, std_dev_watts: ~typing.Dict[str, float] = <factory>, cpu_temperature_c: float | None = None, method: str = 'idle_measurement', metadata: ~typing.Dict[str, ~typing.Any] = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Layer 2 – System idle baseline. NEVER applied to raw data.

This represents the energy the system would consume if completely idle. Used only for derived calculations, never to modify raw measurements.

baseline_id

Unique identifier

Type:

str

timestamp

When baseline was measured

Type:

float

power_watts

Idle power per domain (Watts)

Type:

Dict[str, float]

duration_seconds

How long we measured

Type:

float

sample_count

Number of samples taken

Type:

int

std_dev_watts

Standard deviation per domain

Type:

Dict[str, float]

cpu_temperature_c

Temperature during measurement

Type:

float | None

method

How baseline was obtained

Type:

str

metadata

Additional context

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

baseline_id: str
property core_power_w: float

Get core idle power.

cpu_temperature_c: float | None = None
duration_seconds: float
estimate_energy_uj(duration_seconds: float) → Dict[str, int][source]

Estimate idle energy for a given duration.

Parameters:

duration_seconds – Duration to estimate for

Returns:

Estimated idle energy in microjoules per domain

metadata: Dict[str, Any]
method: str = 'idle_measurement'
min_energy_uj(duration_seconds: float) → Dict[str, int][source]

Convert minimum observed baseline watts into µJ for duration.

property min_power_watts: Dict[str, float]

Estimate a lower-bound idle power for realistic baseline.

Math: mean - 2*std_dev ≈ 2nd percentile (assuming normal distribution) - 68% of values fall within mean ± 1σ - 95% of values fall within mean ± 2σ - 2.5% of values are below mean - 2σ - So mean - 2σ represents ~2.5th percentile (conservative low bound)

For non-normal distributions, this still gives a robust lower estimate that eliminates most background noise while staying physically possible.

property package_power_w: float

Get package idle power.

power_watts: Dict[str, float]
sample_count: int
std_dev_watts: Dict[str, float]
timestamp: float
to_dict() → Dict[str, Any][source]

Convert to dictionary for serialization.

to_json() → str[source]

Serialize to JSON.
